title: I Stank Horse

jokes.text: You running away from home, you gonna need some food. You know, bums, they don't like to share.
jokes.setup: Practical advice for running away
jokes.punchline: Casual mention that bums don't share food
jokes.explanation: Good character-specific humor - Will's street knowledge coming through in unexpected context of helping a 5-year-old run away.

jokes.text: Try to stop you? No, man, with you gone that's more food for me, you know. Plus, I get to keep your turtle and that banging little bike you got.
jokes.setup: Nicky expects Will to try to stop him
jokes.punchline: Will points out the benefits of him leaving
jokes.explanation: Continues the reversal effectively - Will's selfish motivations are funny and believable for his character.

jokes.text: I'm 5, you dork.
jokes.setup: Nicky gives elaborate reasons why he's staying that aren't the real reason
jokes.punchline: Simple, honest truth that he's just a little kid
jokes.explanation: Perfect button on the scene. The simple honesty after his elaborate denials is both sweet and hilarious. Excellent economy and structure.

jokes.text: What, you some kind of freak, boy?
jokes.setup: Geoffrey gets on a kiddie ride
jokes.punchline: Will's shocked reaction to the dignified butler acting childish
jokes.explanation: Good character contrast - dignified Geoffrey on a kids' ride creates the absurdity, Will's reaction lands it.

jokes.text: You got yourself a secret life going, don't you, G?
jokes.setup: Geoffrey's gambling expertise
jokes.punchline: Will's knowing observation about Geoffrey's hidden side
jokes.explanation: Good follow-up line that acknowledges the character reveal and adds to the humor.

jokes.text: Not post position five!
jokes.setup: Carlton's obsessive attachment to his horse
jokes.punchline: His dramatic overreaction to trivial racing detail
jokes.explanation: Classic Carlton obsessiveness played for cringe humor. His emotional investment in trivial details is very in-character.

jokes.text: You sure his tail wasn't up in the air at the time?
jokes.setup: Carlton's sentimental story about his horse looking up at him
jokes.punchline: Will suggests the horse was actually defecating
jokes.explanation: Typical Will deflation of Carlton's sentimentality with crude humor. Effective but not particularly original.

jokes.text: How cute, Daddy. I'd have to live there, of course.
jokes.setup: Philip asks if New York is a bit of a drive every morning
jokes.punchline: Ashley's matter-of-fact response that she'd have to live there
jokes.explanation: Good teenage deadpan to father's naive question. Ashley's tone suggests she thinks he's being deliberately obtuse.

jokes.text: Of course you would, muffin. Of course, it's always been my dream to send my underage daughter off to New York unsupervised. Isn't that every parent's dream?
jokes.setup: Ashley wants to move to New York for school
jokes.punchline: Philip's heavy sarcasm about every parent's dream
jokes.explanation: Standard parental sarcasm but delivered well. Philip's protective father character comes through clearly.

jokes.text: What, that be like a six-lane highway?
jokes.setup: Philip says the road to New York goes through him
jokes.punchline: Will makes a fat joke about Philip's size
jokes.explanation: Lazy fat joke but well-timed and economical. Classic Will smart-mouth response to Uncle Phil.

jokes.text: I bet on every horse.
jokes.setup: Hilary claims to have a system that can't miss
jokes.punchline: Her 'system' is to bet on every horse
jokes.explanation: Perfect Hilary logic - she's technically right but completely misses the point of gambling. Great character integration.

jokes.text: He's never been the tallest person in a room before.
jokes.setup: Carlton is in the stables with the jockeys
jokes.punchline: Will explains it's because Carlton is finally the tallest person around
jokes.explanation: Brilliant character-specific humor. Perfectly captures Carlton's height insecurity and his need to feel important somewhere.

jokes.text: No, acting, but I sucked. My old acting partner is head of the school now. Guess he couldn't even direct, huh?
jokes.setup: Frank studied directing at the school
jokes.punchline: He reveals he actually studied acting but was bad, and his even worse partner now runs the school
jokes.explanation: Good self-deprecating humor with a twist about how mediocrity rises in show business. Sharp observation.

jokes.text: Oh, you're smart. You should produce.
jokes.setup: Ashley makes a smart observation about the acting partner
jokes.punchline: Frank's cynical advice about producing being for smart people who can't do creative work
jokes.explanation: Insider joke about the entertainment industry hierarchy. Quick and sharp.

jokes.text: I'll be there at 7. I'll be there at noon. Feel free to have a bagel while you wait.
jokes.setup: Ashley eagerly says she'll be there at 7
jokes.punchline: Frank corrects her that he'll be there at noon, telling her to wait
jokes.explanation: Good show business stereotype - the casual disrespect for eager young talent's time.

jokes.text: Some short stack told me to get out of the paddock area. God, that feels great to say.
jokes.setup: Carlton returns from the stables
jokes.punchline: He's thrilled to finally call someone else short
jokes.explanation: Perfect callback to the jockey joke. Carlton's joy at finally being able to call someone else short is perfectly in character.

jokes.text: He ain't even leave the gate yet, man.
jokes.setup: Carlton thinks his horse is about to make his move
jokes.punchline: Will points out the horse hasn't even started running
jokes.explanation: Good reality check humor - Carlton's delusion vs. Will's practical observation.

jokes.text: Wednesday Bingo is taking a nap.
jokes.setup: Race results being announced
jokes.punchline: Euphemistic way to say the horse performed terribly
jokes.explanation: Great euphemism for a horse that didn't even try. The understatement makes it funnier than saying he lost badly.

jokes.text: Ashley, the man is in show business, he wouldn't lie.
jokes.setup: Ashley asks if Frank really meant his offer to help
jokes.punchline: Will's sarcastic comment about show business people being honest
jokes.explanation: Good sarcastic observation about show business dishonesty. Will's street smarts showing through.

jokes.text: It's only a day away
jokes.setup: Vet says the horse will be put down tomorrow
jokes.punchline: Will continues singing the optimistic lyric about tomorrow
jokes.explanation: Brilliant dark comedy - the ironic juxtaposition of the optimistic song with the death sentence. Perfect timing and very bold for a family sitcom.

jokes.text: You bastard!
jokes.setup: Carlton is upset about his horse, then Mister Ed comes on TV
jokes.punchline: Carlton's emotional outburst at the TV horse
jokes.explanation: Great emotional release - Carlton's pain about his own horse getting projected onto the cheerful TV horse. Very economical and effective.

jokes.text: Oh, my God. You been sipping that gin and juice.
jokes.setup: Hilary's bizarre commercial behavior
jokes.punchline: Will's assumption she must be drunk
jokes.explanation: Good reaction to Hilary's absurdity. The 'gin and juice' reference is very Will Smith era-appropriate.

jokes.text: Will attempts the commercial: 'And when they do, don't sweat, y'all. Drink Jet.'
jokes.setup: Will thinks the commercial looks easy
jokes.punchline: He adds his own street vernacular to the script
jokes.explanation: Good character work - Will can't help but put his own spin on the corporate script.

jokes.text: Well, then, be a woman on the go. Leave so I can vacuum.
jokes.setup: Will is practicing the women's commercial
jokes.punchline: Geoffrey's dry dismissal incorporating the commercial language
jokes.explanation: Excellent Geoffrey line - perfectly integrates the commercial language into his dismissive butler routine. Great character writing.

jokes.text: You mean, you just say it and they do it? Okay, girls... get naked.
jokes.setup: Will is amazed at director's power over the models
jokes.punchline: He immediately tries to abuse that power
jokes.explanation: Predictable teenage boy humor but fits Will's character. The escalation to 'Simon says' makes it slightly better.

jokes.text: Uh... Simon says, get naked.
jokes.setup: Will's first command didn't work
jokes.punchline: He tries adding 'Simon says' to make it official
jokes.explanation: Good escalation on the previous joke. The childlike logic of 'Simon says' makes it funnier.

jokes.text: And I ain't talking about the soda.
jokes.setup: Will complains about Ashley showing 'that can' on TV
jokes.punchline: He clarifies he doesn't mean the soda can
jokes.explanation: Crude double entendre that's predictable but economically delivered. Typical Will inappropriate humor.

jokes.text: He'll blame Will like he always does. Oh, okay.
jokes.setup: Question about what happens if Dad finds the horse
jokes.punchline: Hilary matter-of-factly states Will always gets blamed
jokes.explanation: Good running gag about Will being the scapegoat. Hilary's casual acceptance makes it funnier.

jokes.text: Yeah, even though they're not showing that much of her face.
jokes.setup: Discussion about the commercial showing Ashley
jokes.punchline: Will implies they're focusing on her body, not her face
jokes.explanation: Another crude observation that's predictable. Will's inappropriate humor is in character but not particularly clever.

jokes.text: That's only because I have trouble sleeping and the sound of the waves gently caressing the shore helps relax me.
jokes.setup: Philip is accused of watching Baywatch
jokes.punchline: His elaborate, poetic excuse for watching a show known for attractive women in swimwear
jokes.explanation: Great character moment - Philip's pompous intellectualization of watching a lowbrow show. The flowery language makes it funnier.

jokes.text: Nice cover. Nice cov...
jokes.setup: Philip's elaborate Baywatch excuse
jokes.punchline: Will starts to mock him but catches himself
jokes.explanation: Good moment of Will catching himself before going too far. Shows character growth and the family dynamic.

jokes.text: There, I said it, your dominance.
jokes.setup: Will is pumping up Philip's masculine authority
jokes.punchline: His dramatic declaration about saying 'dominance'
jokes.explanation: Will's manipulation of Philip's ego. The theatrical delivery of 'dominance' adds to the humor.

jokes.text: Woof, woof, woof. You are the king of the jungle, Mufasa.
jokes.setup: Will is building up Philip's authority
jokes.punchline: He compares him to the Lion King with barking sounds
jokes.explanation: Good escalation with the Lion King reference (very contemporary for the 90s). The barking is absurd but effective.

jokes.text: Hi, sweetie, you look awfully pretty.
jokes.setup: Philip is asserting his dominance and authority
jokes.punchline: He immediately becomes gentle and submissive when Vivian appears
jokes.explanation: Perfect comedic deflation - Philip's 'dominance' instantly evaporates when his wife appears. Great character consistency.

jokes.text: You ain't nothing but Simba.
jokes.setup: Philip's authority crumbles in front of Vivian
jokes.punchline: Will demotes him from Mufasa to Simba (the cub)
jokes.explanation: Brilliant callback that completes the Lion King metaphor. Perfect character observation about the family power dynamic.

jokes.text: You got into him. You're the rat that dimed me out.
jokes.setup: Ashley confronts Will about her situation
jokes.punchline: She calls him a rat for telling on her
jokes.explanation: Standard sibling conflict dialogue. Ashley's anger is justified but the humor is basic.

jokes.text: You don't like rat. How about snake? Weasel? Doody-head?
jokes.setup: Will objects to being called a rat
jokes.punchline: Ashley offers alternative insults, ending with childish 'doody-head'
jokes.explanation: Good escalation that shows Ashley's age - starts mature then devolves to playground insults.

jokes.text: Don't be calling me no doody-head.
jokes.setup: Ashley calls Will 'doody-head'
jokes.punchline: Will takes offense at the childish insult
jokes.explanation: Good reaction - Will can handle serious insults but draws the line at childish name-calling. Perfect character logic.

jokes.text: I'm 5, you dork.
jokes.setup: Will continues seeking validation from Nicky
jokes.punchline: Nicky repeats his signature line from the opening
jokes.explanation: Perfect callback that brings the episode full circle. Shows Nicky's consistent wisdom and Will's tendency to overthink.

jokes.text: Well, Uncle Phil, man. How long you been standing there? You know, you're a light stepper for a big fella.
jokes.setup: Philip caught Will talking about Ashley's rebellion
jokes.punchline: Will's nervous compliment about Philip's stealth despite his size
jokes.explanation: Good nervous humor - Will trying to deflect with awkward compliments. The fat joke is gentle and character-appropriate.

jokes.text: The king of the jungle?
jokes.setup: Philip remembers Will's earlier manipulation
jokes.punchline: He throws Will's own words back at him sarcastically
jokes.explanation: Excellent callback showing Philip isn't as easily manipulated as Will thought. The ironic delivery is perfect.

jokes.text: Big man, short fuse. Bad combination.
jokes.setup: Philip is getting angry
jokes.punchline: Will recalls his own earlier warning about Philip
jokes.explanation: Good callback to Will's earlier assessment of Philip. Shows Will's growing awareness of the situation he created.

jokes.text: Shut up, Will.
jokes.setup: Will tries to make Philip feel worse about not trusting Ashley
jokes.punchline: Philip's blunt dismissal
jokes.explanation: Perfect economical response. Philip's embarrassment and irritation with Will's gloating is perfectly captured.

jokes.text: Yep. You better make it home before Saved by the Bell starts.
jokes.setup: Continued condescension toward Ashley
jokes.punchline: Reference to teen show implying Ashley is too young
jokes.explanation: Good period-specific reference that shows the model's dismissive attitude. Sets up Philip's protective response.

jokes.text: So maybe you and your little horsehair weave should just mosey on... before I get really upset.
jokes.setup: Philip defends Ashley from the rude model
jokes.punchline: His specific insult about her fake hair and gentle threat
jokes.explanation: Great character moment - Philip being protective but trying to stay classy. The 'horsehair weave' is a good specific insult.

jokes.text: Better some horsehair than some no hair.
jokes.setup: Philip insults the model's fake hair
jokes.punchline: She fires back with a bald joke
jokes.explanation: Predictable comeback but economical. Standard insult escalation that moves the plot forward.

jokes.text: And, by the way, your mother.
jokes.setup: Philip and Ashley are celebrating her keeping the commercial job
jokes.punchline: Philip adds a 'your mama' joke as an afterthought
jokes.explanation: Great character moment - dignified Philip stooping to playground insults. The delayed delivery makes it funnier.

jokes.text: Mama. It's 'mama,' Ashley. Mama. It's 'your mama.'
jokes.setup: Philip's attempt at a 'your mama' joke
jokes.punchline: Ashley corrects his technique
jokes.explanation: Excellent callback - Ashley coaching her dignified father in proper insult delivery. Perfect character reversal.

jokes.text: Hey, except for that cat girl. She had a beard.
jokes.setup: Will talks about Ashley's new model friends
jokes.punchline: His observation about one model's facial hair
jokes.explanation: Standard crude observational humor. Gets a laugh but nothing special in terms of craft.

jokes.text: I moved out of the horse when I was 17.
jokes.setup: Family discussion about Ashley moving to New York
jokes.punchline: Vivian's slip saying 'horse' instead of 'house'
jokes.explanation: Sets up the reveal about the hidden horse. The slip works as both a mistake and foreshadowing.

jokes.text: Want me to get you some Pepto-Bismol, Uncle Phil?
jokes.setup: The horse reveal causes chaos
jokes.punchline: Will's practical offer of stomach medicine
jokes.explanation: Good character moment - Will's practical response to Uncle Phil's shock. Shows his street-smart nature.

jokes.text: You can relate to that, Uncle Phil.
jokes.setup: Discussion about it being hard to move the horse's 'girth'
jokes.punchline: Will makes another fat joke about Philip
jokes.explanation: Another lazy fat joke but it's brief and typical Will behavior. Not particularly clever but gets a reaction.

jokes.text: He did it. He did it. What? Hey.
jokes.setup: The family tries to explain the horse situation
jokes.punchline: Everyone immediately blames Will
jokes.explanation: Good callback to the earlier joke about Will always getting blamed. Perfect ending chaos with everyone pointing fingers.

tmdb_overview: Philip expects Ashley to decline an offer for what he considers a degrading role in an ad by a director who could also give her a recommendation to a top New York school.
